1048	  rw [h1, dToCech_comm]
1049	  funext σ
1050	  exact (fLoc_apply s M r (m + 1) (dDiff s M m t) σ).symm
1051	
1052	/-- `IsLocalizedModule.map` of `dDiff` is the bundled localised differential
1053	`locDiff` — by `IsLocalizedModule.ext`, the comparison reducing to `locDiff_fLoc`.
1054	Stated for an arbitrary away element `a` (with `s r = a`) so the spanning-element
1055	bookkeeping in `dDiff_exact` needs no `↑ρ`-rewrite inside the localised map. -/
1056	lemma map_dDiff_eq_locDiff (r : ι) (m : ℕ) {a : R}
1057	    [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) (fLoc s M r m)]
1058	    [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) (fLoc s M r (m + 1))] :
1059	    IsLocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers a) (fLoc s M r m) (fLoc s M r (m + 1))
1060	        (dDiff s M m) = locDiff s M r m := by
1061	  apply IsLocalizedModule.ext (Submonoid.powers a) (fLoc s M r m)
1062	    (fun x => IsLocalizedModule.map_units (fLoc s M r (m + 1)) x)
1063	  apply LinearMap.ext; intro t
1064	  rw [LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply, IsLocalizedModule.map_apply, locDiff_fLoc]
1065	
1066	/-- A chosen `ι`-index realising a spanning-set element as `s i`, kept opaque so
1067	the spanning-element rewrite in `dDiff_exact` has a type-correct motive (the index
1068	must not syntactically contain `↑ρ`). -/
1069	private noncomputable def spanIdx (ρ : ↑(Set.range s)) : ι := ρ.2.choose
1070	
1071	omit [CommRing R] in
1072	private lemma spanIdx_spec (ρ : ↑(Set.range s)) : s (spanIdx s ρ) = ↑ρ := ρ.2.choose_spec
1073	
1074	/-- **Step (a): positive-degree exactness of the un-localised section Čech module
1075	complex `D•`** (`lem:cech_acyclic_affine`, the `R`-module core).  For a spanning
1076	family `s : ι → R` (`Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤`), the complex
1077	`∏_σ M_{s_σ}` is exact in positive degrees.  Reduced — via
1078	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` localising at each spanning element `s_r` — to the
1079	exactness `locDiff_exact` of the localised complexes (which is the dependent
1080	combinatorial core `CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact`), through the localised-
1081	differential identification `map_dDiff_eq_locDiff`. -/
1082	lemma dDiff_exact [Finite ι] (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤) (m : ℕ) :
1083	    Function.Exact (dDiff s M (m + 1)) (dDiff s M (m + 2)) := by
1084	  classical
1085	  haveI inst1 : ∀ ρ : ↑(Set.range s),
1086	      IsLocalizedModule.Away (↑ρ) (fLoc s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 1)) := fun ρ => by
1087	    rw [← spanIdx_spec s ρ]; infer_instance
1088	  haveI inst2 : ∀ ρ : ↑(Set.range s),
1089	      IsLocalizedModule.Away (↑ρ) (fLoc s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 2)) := fun ρ => by
1090	    rw [← spanIdx_spec s ρ]; infer_instance
1091	  haveI inst3 : ∀ ρ : ↑(Set.range s),
1092	      IsLocalizedModule.Away (↑ρ) (fLoc s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 3)) := fun ρ => by
1093	    rw [← spanIdx_spec s ρ]; infer_instance
1094	  refine exact_of_isLocalized_span (Set.range s) hs
1095	    (fun ρ => ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, cechCoeff s M (spanIdx s ρ) σ)
1096	    (fun ρ => fLoc s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 1))
1097	    (fun ρ => ∀ σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι, cechCoeff s M (spanIdx s ρ) σ)
1098	    (fun ρ => fLoc s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 2))
1099	    (fun ρ => ∀ σ : Fin (m + 3) → ι, cechCoeff s M (spanIdx s ρ) σ)
1100	    (fun ρ => fLoc s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 3))
1101	    (dDiff s M (m + 1)) (dDiff s M (m + 2)) fun ρ => ?_
1102	  rw [map_dDiff_eq_locDiff s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 1),
1103	    map_dDiff_eq_locDiff s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 2)]
1104	  exact locDiff_exact s M (spanIdx s ρ) m
1105	
1106	end SectionCechModule
1107	
1108	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — quasi-coherent sections as away localisations (L1, step (b))
1109	
1110	The categorical→module bridge needs the section-identification of
1111	`def:qcoh_sections_localized`: over a basic open `D(g)` the sections of a
1112	quasi-coherent sheaf are the away localisation `M_g`, and the restriction maps
1113	between basic opens are the canonical localisation comparison maps.  For the
1114	standard sheaf `tilde M` this is *verbatim* from Mathlib's `Tilde` development
1115	(`AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen` carries `IsLocalizedModule (.powers g)` and the
1116	restriction compatibility is `tilde.toOpen_res`); the only project-local content is
1117	(i) the multi-index intersection `⨅ₖ D(s_{σ k}) = D(s_σ)` identification, which lets
1118	the degree-`p` section group over the `(p+1)`-fold intersection be read as the
1119	localisation `M_{s_σ}` (this is what `lem:section_cech_homology_exact` consumes
1120	degreewise), and (ii) the identification of the abstract presheaf restriction with
1121	`AwayComparison.comparison`, the differential brick.
1122	
1123	For an *arbitrary* quasi-coherent `F` the remaining input is the affine equivalence
1124	`F ≅ tilde(ΓF)` (Stacks 01I8); see `def:qcoh_sections_localized`.  The tilde case
1125	below is the gap-free part that lands the named target. -/
1126	
1127	/-- Splitting an indexed infimum over `Fin (n+1)` into the `0`-th term and the
1128	infimum over the tail.  Lattice-combinatorial helper behind `basicOpen_sprod`. -/
1129	private lemma iInf_fin_succ {α : Type*} [CompleteLattice α] (n : ℕ) (f : Fin (n + 1) → α) :
1130	    (⨅ i, f i) = f 0 ⊓ ⨅ i : Fin n, f i.succ := by
1131	  apply le_antisymm
1132	  · exact le_inf (iInf_le _ 0) (le_iInf fun i => iInf_le _ i.succ)
1133	  · refine le_iInf fun i => ?_
1134	    refine Fin.cases inf_le_left (fun j => le_trans inf_le_right (iInf_le _ j)) i
1135	
1136	/-- **Multi-index basic-open intersection** (geometric input to step (c)): the
1137	`(p+1)`-fold intersection of the basic opens `D(s_{σ k})` of a {\v C}ech multi-index
1138	`σ : Fin n → ι` is the basic open `D(s_σ)` of the product `s_σ = ∏ₖ s_{σ k}`.  This
1139	identifies the section group `F(⨅ₖ D(s_{σ k}))` of `sectionCechCosimplicial` with the
1140	away localisation `M_{s_σ}` of `SectionCechModule.dCoeff`. -/
1141	lemma basicOpen_sprod {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {ι : Type u} (n : ℕ) (s : ι → R) (σ : Fin n → ι) :
1142	    (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)) : (Spec R).Opens)
1143	      = PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (∏ k, s (σ k)) := by
1144	  induction n with
1145	  | zero =>
1146	      rw [show (∏ k, s (σ k)) = 1 from Finset.prod_of_isEmpty _]
1147	      simp only [PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one]
1148	      exact iInf_of_empty _
1149	  | succ m ih =>
1150	      rw [Fin.prod_univ_succ, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_mul, ← ih (fun i => σ i.succ),
1151	        iInf_fin_succ]
1152	      rfl
1153	
1154	/-- **Quasi-coherent sections over a {\v C}ech intersection are an away localisation**
1155	(`def:qcoh_sections_localized`, tilde case — step (b) of the L1 bridge).  For the
1156	standard sheaf `tilde M` of an `R`-module `M`, the section-restriction map from `M`
1157	to the sections over the `(p+1)`-fold basic-open intersection
1158	`⨅ₖ D(s_{σ k}) = D(s_σ)` exhibits that section group as the away localisation
1159	`M_{s_σ}` (`IsLocalizedModule` for `Submonoid.powers (∏ₖ s_{σ k})`).  Combines the
1160	Mathlib instance `IsLocalizedModule (.powers g) (tilde.toOpen M (D g)).hom` with the
1161	intersection identification `basicOpen_sprod`.  This is the degreewise section
1162	identification that `lem:section_cech_homology_exact` consumes; the restriction
1163	compatibility (item (5) of the blueprint definition) is `tilde.toOpen_res` together
1164	with `qcohRestriction_eq_comparison` below. -/
1165	lemma qcohSectionsAwayLocalized {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {ι : Type u}
1166	    (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) {n : ℕ} (s : ι → R) (σ : Fin n → ι) :
1167	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k)))
1168	      (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom := by
1169	  rw [basicOpen_sprod]
1170	  infer_instance
1171	
1172	/-- **Restriction = localisation comparison** (`def:qcoh_sections_localized`, item (5);
1173	the differential brick of step (c)).  For the standard sheaf `tilde M`, the presheaf
1174	restriction map between basic-open section groups `M_a → M_b` (along an inclusion
1175	`D(b) ⊆ D(a)`) is, as an `R`-linear map, the canonical away-localisation comparison
1176	`AwayComparison.comparison` — provided `a` acts invertibly on `M_b` (`Inverts a M_b`,
1177	which holds whenever `a ∣ b`, the {\v C}ech-face case).  Proved by the universal
1178	property `AwayComparison.comparison_unique`: both the restriction and the comparison
1179	are `R`-linear maps that recover `tilde.toOpen M (D b)` after precomposition with
1180	`tilde.toOpen M (D a)` (the restriction does so by `tilde.toOpen_res`).  Summed over
1181	the alternating signs, this identifies the section {\v C}ech differential with the
1182	module differential `SectionCechModule.dDiff`. -/
1183	lemma qcohRestriction_eq_comparison {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) {a b : R}
1184	    (i : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b : (Spec R).Opens) ⟶ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)
1185	    (hb : AwayComparison.Inverts a
1186	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.obj
1187	        (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)))) :
1188	    ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map i.op).hom
1189	      = AwayComparison.comparison (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom
1190	          (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom hb := by
1191	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a)
1192	      (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom := inferInstance
1193	  refine (AwayComparison.comparison_unique
1194	    (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom
1195	    (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom hb _ ?_).symm
1196	  have h := AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen_res M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)
1197	    (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b) i
1198	  exact congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom h
1199	
1200	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — section {\v C}ech homology bridge (L1 steps c, d)
1201	
1202	The categorical→module bridge of `lem:section_cech_homology_exact`: the
1203	`Ab`-valued section {\v C}ech complex `sectionCechComplex` (of `PresheafCech.lean`)
1204	has its degree-`p` object a *categorical product* `∏ᶜ_σ F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` in `Ab`, and
1205	its differential the alternating sum of the {\v C}ech coface restrictions.  These
1206	lemmas (c1)–(c3) move that abstract complex to the concrete localised-module complex
1207	`SectionCechModule.dDiff` (whose positive-degree exactness `dDiff_exact` is step (a)),
1208	and read off homology vanishing. -/
1209	
1210	section SectionCechBridge
1211	
1212	open CategoryTheory.Limits AlgebraicTopology
1213	
1214	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
1215	
1216	/-- **(c1) Element-level product equivalence** (`lem:section_cech_product_equiv`):
1217	the underlying type of the degree-`p` object `∏ᶜ_σ F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` of the section
1218	{\v C}ech cosimplicial object is the dependent product `∏_σ ToType (F(⨅ₖ U (σ k)))`.
1219	Supplied by `CategoryTheory.Limits.Concrete.productEquiv` (the forgetful functor of
1220	`Ab` preserves discrete products). -/
1221	noncomputable def sectionCechProductEquiv {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
1222	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ℕ) :
1223	    ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk p)) ≃
1224	      (∀ σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι, ToType (F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))) :=
1225	  Concrete.productEquiv
1226	    (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
1227	
1228	/-- Coordinate projection of `sectionCechProductEquiv`: the `σ`-component is the
1229	underlying group map of the categorical projection `Pi.π … σ`.  A definitional
1230	restatement of `Concrete.productEquiv_apply_apply`, named so that downstream proofs can
1231	`rw` it without fighting the `Concrete.productEquiv` coercion. -/
1232	lemma sectionCechProductEquiv_apply {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
1233	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ℕ)
1234	    (y : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk p)))
1235	    (σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι) :
1236	    sectionCechProductEquiv U F p y σ
1237	      = ConcreteCategory.hom
1238	          (Pi.π (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) σ) y :=
1239	  Concrete.productEquiv_apply_apply _ y σ
1240	
1241	/-- **Homology-to-exactness reduction** (the `Ab`-side of `lem:section_cech_ab_exact`):
1242	the degree-`(q+1)` homology of the section {\v C}ech complex vanishes once the
1243	underlying group homomorphisms of the two consecutive coface differentials
1244	`objD q`, `objD (q+1)` form an exact sequence.  Pure homological algebra: combines
1245	`exactAt_iff_isZero_homology`, `exactAt_iff'`, and the abelian-group exactness
1246	criterion `ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact`. -/
1247	lemma sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact {ι : Type u}
1248	    (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (q : ℕ)
1249	    (h : Function.Exact
1250	      (ConcreteCategory.hom (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q))
1251	      (ConcreteCategory.hom
1252	        (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1)))) :
1253	    IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology (q + 1)) := by
1254	  rw [← HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff_isZero_homology,
1255	      (sectionCechComplex U F).exactAt_iff' q (q + 1) (q + 2) (by simp) (by simp),
1256	      ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact]
1257	  have hf : (sectionCechComplex U F).d q (q + 1)
1258	      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q :=
1259	    CochainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared _) q
1260	  have hg : (sectionCechComplex U F).d (q + 1) (q + 2)
1261	      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1) :=
1262	    CochainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared _) (q + 1)
1263	  change Function.Exact
1264	      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((sectionCechComplex U F).d q (q + 1)))
1265	      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((sectionCechComplex U F).d (q + 1) (q + 2)))
1266	  rw [hf, hg]
1267	  exact h
1268	
1269	/-- Application of a finite sum of `Ab`-morphisms distributes over the sum. -/
1270	private lemma ab_hom_finsetSum_apply {A B : Ab.{u}} {κ : Type*}
1271	    (s : Finset κ) (f : κ → (A ⟶ B)) (t : ToType A) :
1272	    ConcreteCategory.hom (∑ i ∈ s, f i) t = ∑ i ∈ s, ConcreteCategory.hom (f i) t := by
1273	  classical
1274	  induction s using Finset.induction with
1275	  | empty => simp
1276	  | insert a s ha ih =>
1277	      rw [Finset.sum_insert ha, Finset.sum_insert ha, AddCommGrpCat.hom_add_apply, ih]
1278	
1279	/-- The `i`-th section {\v C}ech face restriction at a multi-index `σ` of the
1280	cosimplicial section object: the presheaf restriction of `F` from the deleted-face
1281	intersection `⨅ₗ U (σ (δ i l))` to the full intersection `⨅ₖ U (σ k)`.  Factored as a
1282	named `def` so the cosimplicial differential (`sectionCech_objD_apply`) refers to it
1283	by name rather than re-elaborating the `homOfLE (le_iInf …)` term. -/
1284	noncomputable def sectionCechFaceRestr {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
1285	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) {q : ℕ} (σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι) (i : Fin (q + 2)) :
1286	    F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ l, U ((σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) l)))
1287	      ⟶ F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))) :=
1288	  F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf
1289	    (fun l => iInf_le _ ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))).op
1290	
1291	/-- **(c2, abstract) Coface match**: the underlying group action of the section
1292	{\v C}ech coface differential `objD q`, read through the product equivalence, is the
1293	alternating sum of the presheaf restriction maps applied to the deleted-face
1294	coordinates — exactly the shape of `SectionCechModule.dDiff`.  This is purely about
1295	the cosimplicial structure of `sectionCechCosimplicial`; no sheaf identification yet. -/
1296	lemma sectionCech_objD_apply {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
1297	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (q : ℕ)
1298	    (t : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk q)))
1299	    (σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι) :
1300	    sectionCechProductEquiv U F (q + 1)
1301	        (ConcreteCategory.hom
1302	          (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q) t) σ
1303	      = ∑ i : Fin (q + 2), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
1304	          ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i)
1305	            (sectionCechProductEquiv U F q t (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) := by
1306	  classical
1307	  rw [sectionCechProductEquiv_apply,
1308	      show ConcreteCategory.hom
1309	            (Pi.π (fun σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) σ)
1310	            (ConcreteCategory.hom
1311	              (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q) t)
1312	          = ConcreteCategory.hom
1313	              (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q
1314	                ≫ Pi.π (fun σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) σ) t
1315	          from rfl,
1316	      AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD, Preadditive.sum_comp, ab_hom_finsetSum_apply]
1317	  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)
1318	  rw [Preadditive.zsmul_comp]
1319	  simp only [AddCommGrpCat.hom_zsmul, AddMonoidHom.smul_apply]
1320	  rw [sectionCechProductEquiv_apply]
1321	  congr 1
1322	  have hmap : (sectionCechCosimplicial U F).map (SimplexCategory.δ i) ≫ Pi.π _ σ
1323	      = Pi.π _ (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) ≫ sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i :=
1324	    Pi.lift_π _ σ
1325	  rw [CosimplicialObject.δ, hmap]
1326	  rfl
1327	
1328	end SectionCechBridge
1329	
1330	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — tilde-bridge and affine vanishing (L1 steps A, B, d)
1331	
1332	The final stretch of the L1 categorical→module bridge for the **tilde sheaf**
1333	`F = ~M`.  The previous section moved the abstract `Ab`-valued section {\v C}ech complex
1334	to the shape of an alternating sum of presheaf restrictions
1335	(`sectionCech_objD_apply`); here we identify, degreewise, those section groups with the
1336	away-localisation coefficients `dCoeff` and the restrictions with the localisation
1337	cofaces `dCoface`, via the per-σ comparison `φ_σ` (`IsLocalizedModule.iso`).  Transporting
1338	the already-proven module exactness `SectionCechModule.dDiff_exact` across the resulting
1339	degreewise additive isomorphism (`Function.Exact.of_ladder_addEquiv_of_exact`) discharges
1340	the underlying-group exactness, which `sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact` turns
1341	into positive-degree homology vanishing of the section {\v C}ech complex
1342	(`sectionCech_affine_vanishing`). -/
1343	
1344	section SectionCechTilde
1345	
1346	open AlgebraicTopology Scheme.Modules SectionCechModule CechLocalized AwayComparison
1347	
1348	variable {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) {ι : Type u} (s : ι → R)
1349	
1350	set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1351	-- raised: the `IsLocalizedModule.ext` reduction over the heavy `modulesSpecToSheaf` section
1352	-- types is defeq-intensive (the accessor-2 `~M` sections do not reduce cheaply).
1353	/-- **Linear-level per-coface naturality** (`R`-linear).  The comparison `φ_σ`
1354	(written here as the raw `IsLocalizedModule.iso ▸ symm`, definitionally `phiL` below)
1355	intertwines the (accessor-2) {\v C}ech face restriction with the away-localisation coface
1356	`dCoface`.  Proved by `IsLocalizedModule.ext`: both composites send the localisation unit
1357	`toOpen M (D s_{σ∘dᵢ})` of `M` to the localisation unit `mkLinearMap (powers s_σ)`, by
1358	`tilde.toOpen_res` (restriction commutes with `toOpen`), `iso_symm_comp`, and
1359	`comparison_apply` (the defining property of `dCoface`).  Stated and proved *before* the
1360	`phiL` abbreviation so its `IsLocalizedModule.ext` elaboration is not slowed by
1361	fold/unfold attempts against `phiL` — keeping it within the heartbeat budget. -/
1362	private lemma phiL_naturality {q : ℕ} (σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι) (i : Fin (q + 2)) :
1363	    haveI := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s σ
1364	    (IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k)))
1365	        (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom).symm.toLinearMap ∘ₗ
1366	        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map
1367	          (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _
1368	            ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))).op).hom
1369	      = (dCoface s M (q + 1) σ i) ∘ₗ
1370	        (haveI := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s (σ ∘ i.succAbove)
1371	         (IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))
1372	          (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k,
1373	            PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))).hom).symm.toLinearMap) := by
1374	  haveI i1 := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s σ
1375	  haveI i2 := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s (σ ∘ i.succAbove)
1376	  -- Abstract the concrete face-restriction map as an opaque `g` BEFORE `IsLocalizedModule.ext`,
1377	  -- so `ext` never whnf's the (heartbeat-heavy) `modulesSpecToSheaf` restriction.  `hg` records
1378	  -- its `toOpen` compatibility (`tilde.toOpen_res`).
1379	  set g := ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map
1380	      (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _
1381	        ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))).op).hom with hg_def
1382	  have hg : ∀ m : M,
1383	      g ((tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))).hom m)
1384	        = (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom m := by
1385	    intro m
1386	    rw [hg_def]
1387	    exact congrFun (congrArg (fun l => (l.hom : _ → _))
1388	      (tilde.toOpen_res M (⨅ l, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen
1389	          (s ((σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) l)))
1390	        (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))
1391	        (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))))) m
1392	  clear_value g
1393	  refine IsLocalizedModule.ext (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))
1394	    (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))).hom
1395	    (fun x => (Inverts.of_dvd (sprod_succAbove_dvd s σ i)
1396	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ)) M)).isUnit_powers x) ?_
1397	  apply LinearMap.ext
1398	  intro m
1399	  -- unfold the `LinearEquiv`-coercion compositions to plain applications (defeq), avoiding
1400	  -- the semilinear-instance mismatch that blocks `rw`/`simp` on `↑(iso).symm ∘ₗ g`
1401	  change (IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k)))
1402	      (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom).symm
1403	        (g ((tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))).hom m))
1404	      = dCoface s M (q + 1) σ i
1405	        ((IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))
1406	          (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))).hom).symm
1407	          ((tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))).hom m))
1408	  -- the two `iso_symm_comp` facts in applied (defeq) form, so `rw` matches the goal
1409	  have e1 : (IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k)))
1410	        (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom).symm
1411	          ((tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom m)
1412	        = LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k))) M m :=
1413	    DFunLike.congr_fun (IsLocalizedModule.iso_symm_comp (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k)))
1414	      (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom) m
1415	  have e2 : (IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))
1416	        (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))).hom).symm
1417	          ((tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))).hom m)
1418	        = LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k))) M m :=
1419	    DFunLike.congr_fun (IsLocalizedModule.iso_symm_comp
1420	      (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))
1421	      (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))).hom) m
1422	  rw [hg m, e1, e2]
1423	  exact (comparison_apply
1424	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s (σ ∘ i.succAbove))) M)
1425	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ)) M) _ m).symm
1426	
1427	/-- The presheaf of `O_{Spec R}`-modules underlying the tilde sheaf `~M`; the section
1428	{\v C}ech complex of the affine vanishing is taken on this presheaf. -/
1429	private noncomputable abbrev tP : (Spec R).PresheafOfModules :=
1430	  (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj (tilde M)
1431	
1432	/-- The standard basic-open cover family `i ↦ D(s i)`. -/
1433	private abbrev tU : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens (Spec R) :=
1434	  fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i)
1435	
1436	/-- The per-σ comparison `R`-linear isomorphism between the (accessor-2 `ModuleCat`)
1437	sections of `~M` over the {\v C}ech intersection `⨅ₖ D(s_{σ k})` and the away
1438	localisation `M_{s_σ} = dCoeff`.  Both localise `M` at `Submonoid.powers (∏ₖ s_{σ k})`
1439	(`qcohSectionsAwayLocalized`), so they are canonically isomorphic via
1440	`IsLocalizedModule.iso`. -/
1441	private noncomputable def phiL {n : ℕ} (σ : Fin n → ι) :
1442	    ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.obj
1443	        (Opposite.op (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))))
1444	      ≃ₗ[R] dCoeff s M σ :=
1445	  haveI := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s σ
1446	  (IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k)))
1447	    (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom).symm
1448	
1449	/-- The additive per-σ comparison `φ_σ`, the underlying `AddEquiv` of `phiL`, stated on
1450	the accessor-1 (`Ab`-valued) section group so it composes with `sectionCechProductEquiv`. -/
1451	private noncomputable def phi {n : ℕ} (σ : Fin n → ι) :
1452	    ToType ((tP M).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))))
1453	      ≃+ dCoeff s M σ :=
1454	  (phiL M s σ).toAddEquiv
1455	
1456	/-- The raw `IsLocalizedModule.iso ▸ symm` underlying `phi` agrees with `phiL` applied —
1457	the definitional bridge used to feed `phiL_naturality` into `phi_naturality`. -/
1458	private lemma phi_eq_phiL {n : ℕ} (σ : Fin n → ι)
1459	    (y : ToType ((tP M).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))))) :
1460	    phi M s σ y = phiL M s σ y := rfl
1461	
1462	/-- **Abstract restriction bridge.** For any inclusion of opens, the accessor-1
1463	(`Ab`-valued) presheaf restriction of `tP = ~M` and the accessor-2 (`ModuleCat`-valued)
1464	restriction of `modulesSpecToSheaf (~M)` agree on underlying functions.  Stated for an
1465	abstract morphism `g` so that the (otherwise heartbeat-exploding) defeq is checked once,
1466	abstractly, rather than on the concrete {\v C}ech inclusion. -/
1467	private lemma restr_bridge (V W : (Spec R).Opens) (g : V ⟶ W)
1468	    (x : ToType ((tP M).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op W))) :
1469	    ConcreteCategory.hom ((tP M).presheaf.map g.op) x
1470	      = (((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map g.op).hom) x := rfl
1471	
1472	set_option maxHeartbeats 1000000 in
1473	-- raised: feeds `phiL_naturality` through the same defeq-heavy section types via `restr_bridge`.
1474	/-- **Additive per-coface naturality** (accessor-1, the form the coface match consumes).
1475	The additive comparison `φ_σ` intertwines the accessor-1 {\v C}ech face restriction
1476	`sectionCechFaceRestr` with the away-localisation coface `dCoface`.  Obtained from the
1477	linear naturality `phiL_naturality` via the accessor bridge `restr_bridge`. -/
1478	private lemma phi_naturality {q : ℕ} (σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι) (i : Fin (q + 2))
1479	    (x : ToType ((tP M).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op
1480	        (⨅ l, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) l)))))) :
1481	    phi M s σ (ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr (tU s) (tP M) σ i) x)
1482	      = dCoface s M (q + 1) σ i (phi M s (σ ∘ i.succAbove) x) := by
1483	  have hb := restr_bridge M
1484	    (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))
1485	    (⨅ l, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) l)))
1486	    (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))) x
1487	  simp only [sectionCechFaceRestr, tU]
1488	  refine Eq.trans (congrArg (phi M s σ) hb) ?_
1489	  exact DFunLike.congr_fun (phiL_naturality M s σ i) x
1490	
1491	/-- **Additive product equivalence** for the degree-`p` section {\v C}ech term: the
1492	underlying `AddEquiv` upgrade of `sectionCechProductEquiv` (additive because each
1493	coordinate is the underlying group map of a categorical projection `Pi.π`). -/
1494	private noncomputable def sectionProdAddEquiv (p : ℕ) :
1495	    ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial (tU s) (tP M)).obj (SimplexCategory.mk p))
1496	      ≃+ (∀ σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι,
1497	            ToType ((tP M).presheaf.obj
1498	              (Opposite.op (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))))) where
1499	  toFun := sectionCechProductEquiv (tU s) (tP M) p
1500	  invFun := (sectionCechProductEquiv (tU s) (tP M) p).symm
1501	  left_inv := (sectionCechProductEquiv (tU s) (tP M) p).left_inv
1502	  right_inv := (sectionCechProductEquiv (tU s) (tP M) p).right_inv
1503	  map_add' x y := by
1504	    funext σ
1505	    simp only [sectionCechProductEquiv_apply, Pi.add_apply]
1506	    exact map_add _ x y
1507	
1508	/-- The degree-`p` comparison `AddEquiv` `ToType(∏ᶜ_σ ~M(D s_σ)) ≃+ ∏_σ M_{s_σ}`: the
1509	additive product equivalence followed by the coordinatewise comparison `φ_σ`.  These are
1510	the vertical maps of the ladder transporting `dDiff`-exactness to `objD`-exactness. -/
1511	private noncomputable def sectionToModuleAddEquiv (p : ℕ) :
1512	    ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial (tU s) (tP M)).obj (SimplexCategory.mk p))
1513	      ≃+ (∀ σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι, dCoeff s M σ) :=
1514	  (sectionProdAddEquiv M s p).trans (AddEquiv.piCongrRight (fun σ => phi M s σ))
1515	
1516	/-- Coordinate description of `sectionToModuleAddEquiv`. -/
1517	private lemma sectionToModuleAddEquiv_apply (p : ℕ)
1518	    (y : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial (tU s) (tP M)).obj (SimplexCategory.mk p)))
1519	    (σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι) :
1520	    sectionToModuleAddEquiv M s p y σ = phi M s σ (sectionCechProductEquiv (tU s) (tP M) p y σ) :=
1521	  rfl
1522	
1523	/-- Reading the inverse comparison coordinatewise: applying `sectionToModuleAddEquiv.symm`
1524	to a module tuple `z` and projecting recovers `φ_τ⁻¹ (z τ)`. -/
1525	private lemma sectionProdEquiv_symm_apply (q : ℕ) (z : ∀ σ : Fin (q + 1) → ι, dCoeff s M σ)
1526	    (τ : Fin (q + 1) → ι) :
1527	    sectionCechProductEquiv (tU s) (tP M) q ((sectionToModuleAddEquiv M s q).symm z) τ
1528	      = (phi M s τ).symm (z τ) := by
1529	  have h : sectionCechProductEquiv (tU s) (tP M) q ((sectionToModuleAddEquiv M s q).symm z)
1530	      = sectionProdAddEquiv M s q ((sectionProdAddEquiv M s q).symm
1531	          ((AddEquiv.piCongrRight (fun σ => phi M s σ)).symm z)) := rfl
1532	  rw [h, AddEquiv.apply_symm_apply]
1533	  rfl
1534	
1535	/-- **(c2 + tilde-bridge) Coface match** (`lem:section_cech_coface_match`): under the
1536	degreewise comparison `sectionToModuleAddEquiv`, the section {\v C}ech coface differential
1537	`objD` matches the away-localisation module differential `dDiff`.  Combines the abstract
1538	cosimplicial unfold `sectionCech_objD_apply` with the per-coface tilde-bridge
1539	`phi_naturality`. -/
1540	private lemma sectionCechCofaceMatch (q : ℕ) (z : ∀ σ : Fin (q + 1) → ι, dCoeff s M σ) :
1541	    sectionToModuleAddEquiv M s (q + 1)
1542	        (ConcreteCategory.hom
1543	          (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial (tU s) (tP M)) q)
1544	          ((sectionToModuleAddEquiv M s q).symm z))
1545	      = dDiff s M (q + 1) z := by
1546	  funext σ
1547	  rw [sectionToModuleAddEquiv_apply, sectionCech_objD_apply, dDiff_apply, map_sum]
1548	  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)
1549	  rw [map_zsmul, sectionProdEquiv_symm_apply, phi_naturality]
1550	  exact congrArg ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • dCoface s M (q + 1) σ i ·)
1551	    (AddEquiv.apply_symm_apply (phi M s (σ ∘ i.succAbove)) (z (σ ∘ i.succAbove)))
1552	
1553	/-- **(c, ladder transport) Underlying-group exactness** of two consecutive section {\v C}ech
1554	coface differentials (`lem:section_cech_ab_exact`).  Transports the `R`-module exactness
1555	`SectionCechModule.dDiff_exact` across the degreewise additive comparison
1556	`sectionToModuleAddEquiv` (the squares are `sectionCechCofaceMatch`) via
1557	`Function.Exact.of_ladder_addEquiv_of_exact`. -/
1558	private lemma sectionCechAbExact [Finite ι] (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤) (q : ℕ) :
1559	    Function.Exact
1560	      (ConcreteCategory.hom
1561	        (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial (tU s) (tP M)) q))
1562	      (ConcreteCategory.hom
1563	        (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial (tU s) (tP M)) (q + 1))) := by
1564	  have sq : ∀ r : ℕ,
1565	      (ConcreteCategory.hom
1566	            (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial (tU s) (tP M)) r)).comp
1567	          (sectionToModuleAddEquiv M s r).symm.toAddMonoidHom
1568	        = (sectionToModuleAddEquiv M s (r + 1)).symm.toAddMonoidHom.comp
1569	            (dDiff s M (r + 1)).toAddMonoidHom := by
1570	    intro r
1571	    apply AddMonoidHom.ext
1572	    intro z
1573	    simp only [AddMonoidHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, AddEquiv.coe_toAddMonoidHom,
1574	      LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe]
1575	    rw [AddEquiv.eq_symm_apply]
1576	    exact sectionCechCofaceMatch M s r z
1577	  exact Function.Exact.of_ladder_addEquiv_of_exact
1578	    (sectionToModuleAddEquiv M s q).symm (sectionToModuleAddEquiv M s (q + 1)).symm
1579	    (sectionToModuleAddEquiv M s (q + 2)).symm (sq q) (sq (q + 1)) (dDiff_exact s M hs q)
1580	
1581	end SectionCechTilde
1582	
1583	/-- **Section {\v C}ech homology vanishes in positive degrees** (`lem:section_cech_homology_exact`),
1584	tilde case `F = ~M`.  Reads off positive-degree homology vanishing of the section {\v C}ech
1585	complex from the underlying-group exactness `sectionCechAbExact` via the homological precursor
1586	`sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact`. -/
1587	theorem sectionCech_homology_exact {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R)
1588	    {ι : Type u} [Finite ι] (s : ι → R) (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤)
1589	    (p : ℕ) (hp : 1 ≤ p) :
1590	    IsZero ((sectionCechComplex (fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i))
1591	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj (tilde M))).homology p) := by
1592	  obtain ⟨q, rfl⟩ : ∃ q, p = q + 1 := ⟨p - 1, by omega⟩
1593	  exact sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact _ _ q (sectionCechAbExact M s hs q)
1594	
1595	/-- **Standard-cover {\v C}ech vanishing on affines, section form** (`lem:cech_acyclic_affine`,
1596	section form).  For a spanning family `s : ι → R` and the tilde sheaf `~M` of an `R`-module
1597	`M`, the section {\v C}ech complex of the associated standard affine cover has vanishing
1598	cohomology in all positive degrees.  This is the named affine-vanishing target; the general
1599	quasi-coherent `F` is reduced to this tilde case via `F ≅ ~(ΓF)` (Stacks 01I8, deferred). -/
1600	theorem sectionCech_affine_vanishing {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R)
1601	    {ι : Type u} [Finite ι] (s : ι → R) (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤)
1602	    (p : ℕ) (hp : 1 ≤ p) :
1603	    IsZero ((sectionCechComplex (fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i))
1604	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj (tilde M))).homology p) :=
1605	  sectionCech_homology_exact M s hs p hp
1606	
1607	end AlgebraicGeometry

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechHigherDirectImage
8	
9	/-!
10	# Presheaf-level Čech section complex (P3b)
11	
12	This file contains the section Čech complex and the free-Yoneda equivalences:
13	- `sectionCechComplex`        — cosimplicial `Ab`-valued section Čech complex
14	                                (`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechComplex}`)
15	- `freeYonedaHomEquiv`        — Hom-set equivalence out of the free presheaf
16	                                (`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaHomEquiv}`)
17	- `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`     — additive version of the above
18	                                (`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaHomAddEquiv}`)
19	- `injective_toPresheafOfModules` — injectivity transport to presheaves of modules
20	                                (`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.injective_toPresheafOfModules}`)
21	
22	Related machinery that has moved to sibling files:
23	- `cechFreePresheafComplex` / `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` → `FreePresheafComplex.lean`
24	- `cechComplex_hom_identification` / `injective_cech_acyclic` → `CechBridge.lean`
25	-/
26	
27	universe u
28	
29	open CategoryTheory Limits
30	
31	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
32	
33	/-
34	Planner strategy (P3b presheaf-Čech bridge; see analogies/p3b-presheafcech.md +
35	blueprint §Presheaf-level Čech machinery):
36	
37	The goal is to prove that injective `O_X`-modules have trivial positive-degree Čech
38	cohomology with respect to any open cover.  The strategy avoids `presheafModules_
39	enoughInjectives` and `cech_delta_functor_presheaves` (decisions 4–5 in the analogy
40	file are expensive Mathlib gaps and are NOT on the critical path).  The build order is:
41	
42	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
43	Step 1 — `sectionCechComplex`
44	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
45	Type: given a scheme `X`, an open cover `𝒰 : X.OpenCover`, an open `U : Opens ↥X`,
46	and a presheaf of modules `F : X.PresheafOfModules`, produce the section Čech
47	cochain complex
48	
49	    Č•(𝒰, F, U) : CochainComplex (ModuleCat (X.ringCatSheaf.val.obj (op U))) ℕ
50	
51	whose degree-`p` term is the product `∏_{(i₀,…,iₚ) : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.J}
52	  F.obj (op (𝒰.U i₀ ⊓ … ⊓ 𝒰.U iₚ ⊓ U))`, equipped with the alternating restriction
53	differential.
54	
55	THIS OBJECT IS DISTINCT from `CechComplex` in `CechHigherDirectImage.lean`:
56	`CechComplex` lives in `S.Modules` (pushforward along `f`) and is a relative complex.
57	`sectionCechComplex` is a plain cochain complex of `R`-modules where `R = O_X(U)`.
58	
59	Key Mathlib hooks:
60	- `PresheafOfModules` — the category `X.PresheafOfModules =
61	  PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.val`.
62	- `PresheafOfModules.evaluation R (op V) : PresheafOfModules R ⥤ ModuleCat (R.obj (op V))`
63	  — the exact "sections over `V`" functor; use it to extract `F.obj (op V)`.
64	- `CochainComplex.of` or `HomologicalComplex.mk` — assemble the complex from
65	  degree-`p` objects and differentials.
66	
67	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
68	Step 2 — `cechFreePresheafComplex`
69	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
70	Type: given `X` and `𝒰`, produce the chain complex of presheaves of modules
71	
72	    K(𝒰)_• : ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ
73	
74	whose degree-`p` term is the direct sum `⨁_{(i₀,…,iₚ) : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.J}
75	  (PresheafOfModules.free _).obj (yoneda.obj (𝒰.U i₀ ⊓ … ⊓ 𝒰.U iₚ))`.
76	
77	DO NOT introduce a bespoke extension-by-zero functor `j_!`.  The blueprint's
78	`(j_{i₀…iₚ})_! (O_X|_{U_{i₀…iₚ}})` is canonically identified with
79	`(PresheafOfModules.free _).obj (yoneda.obj U_…)`: on sections over `V`, this is
80	the free `R(V)`-module on `Hom(V, U_…)` — which is `R(V)` when `V ⊆ U_…` and `0`
81	otherwise, matching extension-by-zero of `O_X|_{U_…}` exactly.
82	
83	The differentials are `(PresheafOfModules.free _).map` of the representable maps
84	`yoneda.obj (U_{i₀⊓…⊓U_{iₚ₊₁}}) → yoneda.obj (U_{i₀⊓…⊓̂ᵢⱼ⊓…⊓U_{iₚ₊₁}})` induced
85	by the inclusion of opens `𝒰.U i₀ ⊓ … ⊓ 𝒰.U iₚ₊₁ ≤ 𝒰.U i₀ ⊓ … ⊓̂ᵢⱼ ⊓ …`, with
86	the standard alternating sign.
87	
88	Key Mathlib hooks:
89	- `PresheafOfModules.free R : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u) ⥤ PresheafOfModules R` — the free
90	  presheaf-of-modules functor.
91	- `PresheafOfModules.freeAdjunction R : free R ⊣ toPresheaf R ⋙ whiskeringRight … (forget Ab)`
92	  — the adjunction; `freeObjDesc` / `freeHomEquiv` give the universal property
93	  `Hom(free(P), F) ≅ NatTrans P (F ∘ forget)` used in step 3.
94	- `yoneda : Opens ↥X ⥤ (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u` — the Yoneda embedding.
95	- Use `Finset.univ`-indexed direct sums; `ChainComplex.of` or `HomologicalComplex.mk`
96	  to assemble.
97	
98	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
99	Step 3 — `cechComplex_hom_identification`
100	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
101	Type: a natural isomorphism of cochain complexes of abelian groups (or
102	`O_X(U)`-modules):
103	
104	    Hom_{X.PresheafOfModules}(K(𝒰)_•, F)  ≅  Č•(𝒰, F, U)
105	
106	i.e., the complex of hom-sets out of `K(𝒰)_•` is the section Čech cochain complex.
107	
108	Strategy:
109	- At degree `p`, term-by-term: use the adjunction iso
110	  `(PresheafOfModules.freeAdjunction _).homEquiv` to identify
111	  `Hom_{PMod}(free(yoneda(U_I)), F)  ≅  NatTrans (yoneda(U_I)) (toPresheaf F)`
112	  and then Yoneda (`yonedaEquiv`) to get `≅  (toPresheaf F).obj (op U_I) = F.obj (op U_I)`.
113	  The product over multi-indices matches the degree-`p` term of `Č•(𝒰, F, U)`.
114	- Check that the differential intertwines: the differential on `Hom(K_•, F)` induced
115	  by pre-composition with the alternating-sign `free.map` maps matches the alternating
116	  restriction differential of `Č•(𝒰, F, U)`.
117	- Build as a cochain-complex isomorphism using `HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents`.
118	
119	Key Mathlib hooks:
120	- `PresheafOfModules.freeAdjunction` — the core adjunction.
121	- `yonedaEquiv` — the Yoneda natural bijection.
122	- `PresheafOfModules.evaluation` — sections extraction.
123	
124	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
125	Step 4 — `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`
126	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
127	Type: the augmented complex `K(𝒰)_• → O_𝒰[0]` (augmentation = "sheaf restricted to
128	the cover") is a quasi-isomorphism, i.e., `K(𝒰)_•` is a free resolution of `O_𝒰`.
129	
130	Strategy (Mathlib has no packaged Čech contractibility lemma; build it directly):
131	- Homology of presheaves of modules is computed objectwise
132	  (limits/colimits in `PresheafOfModules R` are objectwise; see `HasColimits`
133	  instance). So it suffices to show that for each open `V : Opens ↥X`,
134	  the complex `K(𝒰)_•(V)` of `R(V)`-modules is contractible.
135	- The sectionwise complex `K(𝒰)_•(V)` is:
136	  degree `p` = `⨁_{I : Fin(p+1)→J} R(V)` when `V ⊆ U_{I(0)} ⊓ … ⊓ U_{I(p)}`, else `0`.
137	- Fix any index `i_fix` such that `V ⊆ 𝒰.U i_fix` (if no such index, the complex is
138	  `0` and trivially exact). The contracting homotopy `h : K_p(V) → K_{p+1}(V)` is:
139	      `h(s)_{i₀,i₁,…,iₚ} = (i₀ = i_fix) · s_{i₁,…,iₚ}`
140	  (extend the multi-index by prepending `i_fix`). Check: `dh + hd = id` at each degree.
141	- Assemble using `Homotopy` / `HomotopyEquiv` from Mathlib (`HomologicalComplex.Homotopy`
142	  in `Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Homotopy`). From the chain homotopy conclude
143	  `HomotopyEquiv` and hence quasi-isomorphism.
144	- Do NOT route through `ExtraDegeneracy` (it has a different index convention and is
145	  not directly applicable here).
146	
147	Key Mathlib hooks:
148	- `HomologicalComplex.Homotopy` — chain homotopy type.
149	- `HomotopyEquiv.toQuasiIso` (or `Homotopy.toQuasiIso`) — chain homotopy ⟹ quasi-iso.
150	
151	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
152	Step 5 — `injective_cech_acyclic`
153	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
154	Type: for an injective `I : X.Modules` and an open cover `𝒰 : X.OpenCover`, the
155	positive-degree Čech cohomology `Ȟ^p(𝒰, I) = 0` for all `p > 0`.
156	
157	Strategy (two independent parts; does NOT need decisions 4–5):
158	
159	Part (a) — Injective sheaf ⟹ injective presheaf:
160	- Apply `CategoryTheory.Injective.injective_of_adjoint` to the adjunction
161	  `PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction α` where `α = 𝟙` for the scheme `X`.
162	  This adjunction is `sheafification α ⊣ (forget R) ⋙ restrictScalars α`, i.e.
163	  sheafification is left adjoint to the inclusion `toPresheafOfModules`.
164	- The left adjoint `sheafification` is exact (it is exact as a colimit of
165	  sheafification-endofunctors over a Grothendieck topology), hence in particular
166	  `PreservesMonomorphisms`. So `Injective.injective_of_adjoint` gives:
167	  `I` injective in `X.Modules` ⟹ `Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules.obj I`
168	  is injective in `X.PresheafOfModules`.
169	- Relevant Mathlib names:
170	  `PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction`,
171	  `CategoryTheory.Injective.injective_of_adjoint`,
172	  `Functor.preservesInjectiveObjects_of_adjunction_of_preservesMonomorphisms`,
173	  `Adjunction.map_injective`.
174	
175	Part (b) — Vanishing of positive Čech cohomology:
176	- By step 4, `K(𝒰)_• → O_𝒰[0]` is an exact augmented complex (quasi-iso from step 4).
177	- Since `I_pshf := toPresheafOfModules.obj I` is injective in `X.PresheafOfModules`
178	  (by part (a)), the functor `Hom_{PMod}(-, I_pshf)` is exact.
179	- Applying `Hom_{PMod}(-, I_pshf)` to the exact augmented complex `K(𝒰)_• → O_𝒰` and
180	  using step 3 (`cechComplex_hom_identification`) to identify the resulting complex
181	  with `Č•(𝒰, I, U)`, conclude that `Č•(𝒰, I, U)` is exact in positive degrees, i.e.
182	  `Ȟ^p(𝒰, I) = 0` for `p > 0`.
183	- This is the Stacks-project proof of `lemma-injective-trivial-cech` (Stacks tag
184	  0BKP and surrounding; see `references/stacks-cohomology.md`).
185	
186	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
187	Verified Mathlib API summary (LSP-checked in this project):
188	  PresheafOfModules
189	  PresheafOfModules.free
190	  PresheafOfModules.freeAdjunction
191	  PresheafOfModules.evaluation
192	  PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
193	  CategoryTheory.Injective.injective_of_adjoint
194	  yoneda
195	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
196	-/

198	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — PresheafCech (injective transfer) -/
199	
200	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
201	
202	/-- **An injective sheaf of `O_X`-modules is injective as a presheaf of modules.**
203	
204	This is Part 1 of the {\v C}ech-acyclicity of injectives (blueprint
205	`lem:injective_cech_acyclic`, Stacks `lemma-injective-trivial-cech`): the inclusion
206	`Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X : X.Modules ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules` is the right
207	adjoint of the (exact) sheafification functor, so it carries injective objects to
208	injective objects.
209	
210	Project-local because Mathlib has no packaged "injective sheaf of modules ⟹ injective
211	presheaf of modules" statement; it is obtained here by feeding
212	`PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 _)` (whose left adjoint
213	`sheafification` preserves monomorphisms) to `Injective.injective_of_adjoint`. The
214	right adjoint of that adjunction is *definitionally* `toPresheafOfModules X`
215	(`SheafOfModules.forget _ ⋙ restrictScalars (𝟙 _)`). -/
216	theorem injective_toPresheafOfModules (I : X.Modules) [Injective I] :
217	    Injective ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj I) := by
218	  -- the left adjoint of `toPresheafOfModules X` is `sheafification (𝟙 _)`, which is
219	  -- exact and in particular preserves monomorphisms
220	  haveI : (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).PreservesMonomorphisms :=
221	    inferInstance
222	  -- realign the ambient category instance so the `Injective` instance is found cheaply
223	  haveI : Injective (C := SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf) I := ‹Injective I›
224	  exact Injective.injective_of_adjoint
225	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)) I
226	
227	/-- **Free–Yoneda representability (per-term bijection).**
228	
229	For an open `V : Opens X` and a presheaf of `O_X`-modules `F`, the morphisms out of the
230	free presheaf of modules on the representable `yoneda V` are exactly the sections of
231	`F` over `V`:
232	```
233	  Hom_{PMod(O_X)}(free(yoneda V), F) ≃ F(V).
234	```
235	This is the free–forgetful adjunction (`PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv`) composed with
236	the Yoneda bijection (`yonedaEquiv`). It is the single-multi-index core of the
237	{\v C}ech complex hom-identification (blueprint `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`,
238	Stacks `lemma-cech-map-into`): taking the product over multi-indices turns this into
239	`Hom(K(𝒰)_p, F) = Č^p(𝒰, F)`.
240	
241	Project-local because Mathlib packages the two halves separately but not their
242	composite as the corepresentability of evaluation by `free(yoneda V)`. The target
243	`(F.presheaf ⋙ forget Ab).obj (op V)` is the underlying set of the section group
244	`F(V)`. -/
245	noncomputable def freeYonedaHomEquiv (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
246	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
247	    ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) ≃
248	      (F.presheaf ⋙ forget Ab).obj (Opposite.op V) :=
249	  PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv.trans yonedaEquiv
250	
251	/-- **Generator formula for `freeYonedaHomEquiv`.** The bijection
252	`Hom(free(yoneda V), F) ≃ F(V)` is evaluation of the morphism's component at `V` on the
253	canonical free generator `freeMk (𝟙 V)`. Recorded project-locally because this concrete
254	description is what makes the bijection visibly additive (`freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`) and is
255	unavailable as a packaged Mathlib lemma. -/
256	lemma freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules)
257	    (ψ : (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) :
258	    freeYonedaHomEquiv V F ψ
259	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (ψ.app (Opposite.op V))) (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V)) := by
260	  simp only [freeYonedaHomEquiv]; rfl
261	
262	/-- **Additive upgrade of `freeYonedaHomEquiv`.** The free–Yoneda hom bijection
263	`Hom_{PMod}(free(yoneda V), F) ≃ F(V)` is an isomorphism of abelian groups: the source is
264	the (preadditive) hom-group of presheaves of modules and the target is the section group
265	`F(V)`. This is the per-multi-index additive core of the {\v C}ech hom-identification
266	(blueprint `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`); taking products over multi-indices and
267	intertwining with the differential upgrades it to the full complex isomorphism. Additivity
268	holds because, by `freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply`, the bijection is evaluation of the
269	component `ψ.app (op V)` (a module map, hence additive) on a fixed generator, and the
270	component of a sum of morphisms is the sum of components.
271	
272	Project-local because Mathlib packages the underlying bijection (`freeHomEquiv` ∘
273	`yonedaEquiv`) only at the level of types, not as an `AddEquiv`. -/
274	noncomputable def freeYonedaHomAddEquiv (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
275	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
276	    ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) ≃+
277	      (F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)) where
278	  toFun := freeYonedaHomEquiv V F
279	  invFun := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).symm
280	  left_inv := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).left_inv
281	  right_inv := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).right_inv
282	  map_add' f g := by
283	    rw [freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply,
284	      PresheafOfModules.add_app]
285	    change (ModuleCat.Hom.hom (f.app (Opposite.op V) + g.app (Opposite.op V))) _ = _
286	    rw [ModuleCat.hom_add]; rfl
287	
288	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — section {\v C}ech complex -/
289	
290	/-- **Cosimplicial abelian group underlying the section {\v C}ech complex.**
291	
292	For a family of opens `U : ι → Opens X` (an open covering) and a presheaf of modules `F`,
293	the cosimplicial object in `Ab` whose value in cosimplicial degree `p` is the product
294	`∏_{σ : Fin (p+1) → ι} F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` of sections over the `(p+1)`-fold intersections. A
295	cosimplicial map `f : [m] ⟶ [n]` acts at multi-index `σ : Fin (n+1) → ι` by projecting to
296	the coordinate `σ ∘ f` and then restricting along `⨅ₖ U (σ k) ≤ ⨅ₗ U (σ (f l))`. This is the
297	covariant ({\v C}ech) variance that the alternating-coface-map complex turns into a cochain
298	complex.
299	
300	Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules. Built
301	as an honest `SimplexCategory ⥤ Ab` functor so that `alternatingCofaceMapComplex` supplies
302	`d² = 0` for free (no hand-rolled alternating-sum identity). -/
303	noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicial {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
304	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} where
305	  obj n := ∏ᶜ (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
306	  map {m n} f := Pi.lift (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
307	    Pi.π _ (σ ∘ f.toOrderHom) ≫
308	      F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ (f.toOrderHom l)))).op)
309	  map_id n := by
310	    apply Pi.hom_ext
311	    intro σ
312	    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp, SimplexCategory.id_toOrderHom,
313	      OrderHom.id_coe, Function.comp_id]
314	    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE _).op (𝟙 _)]
315	    simp
316	  map_comp {l m n} f g := by
317	    apply Pi.hom_ext
318	    intro σ
319	    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom, OrderHom.comp_coe]
320	    rw [← Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
321	    congr 1
322	
323	/-- **Section {\v C}ech cochain complex of a presheaf of modules**
324	(blueprint `def:section_cech_complex`).
325	
326	The cochain complex `Č•(𝒰, F)` of abelian groups with degree-`p` term
327	`∏_{σ : Fin (p+1) → ι} F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` and the alternating-sum-of-restrictions differential,
328	obtained as the alternating-coface-map complex of `sectionCechCosimplicial`. This is the
329	section complex of the blueprint, kept distinct from the relative pushforward `CechComplex`;
330	it is the right-hand side of the {\v C}ech hom-identification
331	`Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)`.
332	
333	Project-local: no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules exists in Mathlib. -/
334	noncomputable def sectionCechComplex {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
335	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CochainComplex Ab.{u} ℕ :=
336	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).obj (sectionCechCosimplicial U F)
337	
338	end AlgebraicGeometry

17	  — the chain complex of free presheaves of `O_X`-modules whose degree-`p` term is
18	  `⨁_{σ : Fin(p+1) → ι} (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj`
19	  `  (yoneda.obj (⨅ k, U (σ k)))`.

21	- `AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`  (`lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso`)
22	  — the augmentation `cechFreeComplexAug : K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]` is a quasi-isomorphism / the free
23	  complex is a free resolution of `O_𝒰`.  **(built and axiom-clean.)**  The proof reduces objectwise
24	  (`quasiIso_of_evaluation`) to: the empty case `cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_isEmpty`; and the nonempty
25	  case `cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_nonempty`, which transfers the engine quasi-isomorphism
26	  `cechEngineComplexAug_quasiIso` across `cechFreeEvalEngineIso`, using the degree-`0` augmentation
27	  identification `cechFreeAug_eval_eq` and the geometric identification
28	  `coverStructurePresheafEval_iso`.
29	
30	The quasi-isomorphism is proved objectwise (`quasiIso_of_evaluation`): for each open `V`,
31	the evaluated augmented complex is the augmented combinatorial Čech complex of the full
32	simplex on `I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}` with constant coefficients `O_X(V)`, which is
33	contractible when `I₁(V) ≠ ∅` (via the prepend-`i_fix` homotopy
34	`FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy`) and zero when `I₁(V) = ∅`.  This file owns the
35	combinatorial engine (`FreeCechEngine`) supplying that contracting homotopy and the

46	Planner strategy (P3b free-complex side; see analogies/p3b-presheafcech.md +
47	blueprint §Presheaf-level Čech machinery):
48	
49	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
50	`cechFreePresheafComplex`
51	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
52	Goal: a `ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ` whose degree-`p` term is
53	  `⨁_{σ : Fin(p+1) → ι} (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj (⨅ k, U (σ k)))`
54	with the alternating-face differential.
55	
56	Recommended build path — use the simplicial route so that d²=0 comes for free:
57	1. Construct a `SimplicialObject X.PresheafOfModules` whose `n`-simplices are the
58	   above direct sums.
59	2. Apply `AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex` to obtain the chain complex.
60	   This gives d²=0 automatically via `SimplicialObject.boundarySquareZero`.
61	
62	Key API:
63	- `PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj :`
64	  `  ((Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u) ⥤ PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.obj`
65	  — the free-presheaf-of-modules functor.  Use it as `(PresheafOfModules.free _).obj` and
66	  `(PresheafOfModules.free _).map`.
67	- `yoneda.obj V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u` — the representable presheaf of sets at `V`.
68	  Do NOT introduce a bespoke `j_!`; `free ∘ yoneda` is the correct substitute.
69	- `AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex` — turns a simplicial abelian group (or
70	  simplicial object in an abelian category) into a chain complex; d²=0 is a theorem.
71	- Direct sums: `⨁` is `DirectSum`; in `PresheafOfModules` use
72	  `Limits.biproduct` / `DirectSum.lof` / `DirectSum.desc`.
73	
74	DEAD END — do NOT hand-roll the alternating-sum identity for d²=0.  Use the simplicial
75	path above.
76	
77	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
78	`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`
79	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
80	Goal: show that `cechFreePresheafComplex` → `O_𝒰[0]` is a quasi-isomorphism (i.e.,
81	the free complex is a free resolution of the structure sheaf restricted to the cover).
82	
83	Recommended build path — objectwise contracting homotopy:
84	1. Homology in `X.PresheafOfModules` is computed objectwise (colimits are objectwise).
85	   So it suffices to exhibit, for each open `V : Opens ↥X`, a contracting homotopy on
86	   the sectionwise complex `cechFreePresheafComplex(V)`.
87	2. Sectionwise at `V`, degree `p` is `⨁_{σ : Fin(p+1) → ι} R(V)` for those multi-indices
88	   `σ` with `V ⊆ ⨅ k, U (σ k)`, and `0` otherwise.
89	3. Fix any `i_fix : ι` with `V ⊆ U i_fix` (if none exists, the complex is `0`).
90	   The homotopy `h : K_p(V) → K_{p+1}(V)` maps the `σ`-summand to the `(i_fix, σ)`-summand
91	   (prepend `i_fix`).  Check `dh + hd = id` at each degree.
92	4. Package as `HomologicalComplex.Homotopy`, then use `HomotopyEquiv.toQuasiIso`.
93	
94	Key API:
95	- `HomologicalComplex.Homotopy` — `Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Homotopy`.
96	- `HomotopyEquiv.toQuasiIso` (or `Homotopy.toQuasiIso`) — homotopy equivalence ⟹ quasi-iso.
97	
98	DEAD END — do NOT route through `SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy`.  That
99	interface has a different index convention and is not directly applicable here.
100	-/
